Second, I bought some coconut at the market, and I found that some of it was spoiled. I had diarrhea that night and had to get up three or four times.
Third, the floor tiles next to the toilet were damp. When I stepped on them, water splashed onto my foot.
Fourth, the blanket was too thick and heavy, making it very uncomfortable. They prepared it as if it were winter.
Catinina was very professional and handled the issue quickly. She made five phone calls, obtained authorization, and gave me a receipt. I hope I can receive a refund of 370 CAD.

reviews.title: Westin Hotel & Resort Cancun
reviews.text: For me, this was not a good location in Cancun. The hotel is built too close to the beach which makes the beach very narrow. The beach by the Westin Lagunamar is much nicer for me and what I look to for at a beach resort. There are no steps to enter or exit the main pool. You can only enter/exit by side ladders. There were several elderly people who could not climb the ladders to exit the pool which created some chaos and there were was no supervision at the pool to help these people. There were other dangerous area's around the pool/beach entrance. In my opinion, the Hotel needs to completely remodel this area. The hotel was very crowded for the weekend and it appeared that they were understaffed. I felt like the staff was only interested in getting us to take a timeshare presentation that make us feel like we were on vacation. The hotel does not have a hot tub open to it's guests. There is a whirlpool but it is the same temperature as the pool. There is a hot tub in the Spa but you have to pay extra to enter the spa to use it. So this was very disappointing to me.

reviews.title: I will stay away from the Westin brand. Low end hotels or camping are far more comfortable.
reviews.positives: The breakfast was good. There was not much else to like.
reviews.negatives: The AC was so loud we could not keep it on and slept with it being off. The phone did not work so we could not call the desk to complain. There was no hot water in the shower and I had to shower with cold water. My partner chose not to shower at all. The distance from the lobby to the elevator was long. The three corridors we passed felt like an airport terminal - but without signage. We had to ask for directions 3 times. The floor numbers were not marked by the elevator. We could not tell which floor we reached when the door opened. Numbers painted on the door jamb could help. Room furnishing was sparse. The commode was so low that sitting on it caused unsanitary interactions with the water below... For $385/night we could expect for better. We complained at check out and all the associate could say was that he was "sorry". Do you ever do more than just be "sorry"?

reviews.title: Surly staff and poor service
reviews.text: I booked the hotel as an overnight transit stop en route from Havana to Dallas. I was held up at check in because staff could not locate my booking. I had the district feeling that the reception staffer thought that I was trying to dupe him. He eventually found the booking and gave me a key. I took myself to the room (no bell-hop to escort or help with luggage) Not a great start. The room was fine - more than I needed for my overnight stopover. My real beef arose when I enquired about my return to the airport with the hotel shuttle. I was told that I could not use this service as it has a minimum 24 hour pre-booking. When I explained that I would be resident in the hotel for one night only I was offered a shrug of the shoulders. The cost for the hotel taxi for the 8 minute ride to the airport was US$35. Not impressed.

reviews.text:
The hotel itself is amazing and the most staffs are also great but I don't recommend the place because of a horrible incident.
My female friend arrived a day earlier than us and while she was having dinner alone, the waiter left his phone number on the bill. That would've been okay but he kept calling her room at night and made her really freaked out. She was so scared that when we arrived next day and called her room, she didn't pick up. That evening the guy called again and asking her why she didn't go for dinner!! We were far away from Europe and didn't know how much help we could get from police so didn't make any official complaint. I just want to say it again, it looks a great place but might not be 100% safe for female travelers

reviews.title: Great Location!
reviews.text:
The proximity to the airport and the ease in getting around makes this a great choice.  The hotel itself is dated.  The rooms is a nice size and the best features are the ocean view and the comfortable bed. 

As a first time visitor to Cancun and this hotel, I would have appreciated being told that I had to “request” that the credit card that I provided at check-in did not guarantee that I could bill charges to my room.  I needed to request that???  Also, NONE of the restaurants/services accepted cash or pesos.  Everything had to be charged?!  I paid a fee for that.  Disappointing!

Lastly, a concierge helped book a tour that had been closed for over a week.  Something we learned the morning of our scheduled tour.  We were fortunate enough to find our own tour that same day.

All-in-all, we enjoyed the visit and will come back…to Cancun.

reviews.title: Low end property with high prices,very noisy rooms,not adequately furnished, bar food only
reviews.positives: On a bright note - the ocean and the beach were spectacular (when you could find an open beach chair).
reviews.negatives:
We were booked on the first floor which is the “service delivery highway” for the resort and provided 24/7 of extreme noise - banging, large tin carts rumbling on tiled flooring (only floor w rooms w/o carpet in hallway), late night deliveries w banging doors and staff loudly communicating w each other.
This floor should not have guest rooms.
The rooms were sparsely furnished and had obvious pieces of furniture missing making it harder to find space for personal items during our stay.
We finally were able to move to a different floor (after 5 days) and though it was a bit quieter it was even more sparsely furnished requiring some of our personal items to remain on the floor.

The food was ‘low end bar food’ unless you booked their very pricey ‘reservation only’ restaurant.
There were little to no healthy options (iceberg lettuce for most salads) and everything was previously frozen and delivered luke warm.  Very little (if anything) to accommodate food allergies.
Prices did not reflect the low end food provided - way over priced.
It presented as a low end property trying to be better than it was - with very high prices for food & drink.

reviews.title: Underwhelming
reviews.positives: It was convenient and easy for one last night in Cancun before heading to the airport. The staff who helped us book taxis etc were super friendly and wonderful but sadly let down by others.
reviews.negatives: All the outdoor amenities were fantastic don’t get me wrong, however the inside of the hotel especially the corridors and rooms could have used some love and attention. The bath wasn’t very nice and the bath plug didn’t actually fit anyways so (I appreciate it’s weird in the heat but with a sore back) we couldn’t have a bath.  We booked our room 6 months before coming with breakfast included for 3 adults staying. On check in we were reminded we had booked breakfast by the man who checked us in! Sadly the breakfast wasn’t great and one of us had one piece of French toast, a slice of melon and a black coffee. Upon leaving we were asked to pay for a breakfast (nearly $30CAD!!!) as apparently our room only came with 2 breakfasts included which was incredibly strange given the booking had always been for 3 people and we were reminded of breakfast. We questioned it and were treated quite rudely by who I can only imagine was the hotel manager. We were directed to some very fine print in a different part of our booking that said it was only 2 breakfasts on the room. This is clearly something that happens a lot as this was not obvious but they seemed to know exactly what the issue was. We felt incredibly scammed. He offered us 50% off the cost of the breakfast and accused us of lying when we explained that one of our party had barely eaten. Sadly, it was such a bitter experience it meant one of our party who was going to stay for the day, have some lunch and had a massage booked, decided to leave and spend 8 hours at the airport instead rather than spend any more money at the chain. The whole thing felt incredibly sad at the end of a lovely holiday where we had stayed at some wonderful hotels. Some of the staff were wonderful and lovely and I feel bad being negative but feel I need to make people aware.

reviews.title: Value for money
reviews.text:
Location: The hotel has direct access to the beach which was great. The R1/R2 bus stop was right outside the hotel so ideal for travellers looking to use public transportation. However, location is slightly secluded and not too many restaurants nearby.

Staff: The staff were friendly and accommodating. Upon request, they even let us do late check out without any charge. Shout-out to our concierge Richie and Masseur Yuri for their great service.  

Facilities: I found no significant issues issues with the hotel facilities or amenities.  The AC somehow felt like it was not cooling properly on the first day but the issue got resolved later.  The hotel itself is old and definitely could do with renovations but that wasn't a deal breaker for us.

Food:The food was pretty great and decently priced esp at Arena sports bar. Buffet breakfast at Sabor restaurant had a good selection of food as well. They also give you a list outside restaurants where you can avail 10% discount or welcome drinks if you mention you are staying at Westin. I recommend checking out Fred's and Navios from that list. 

Other facilities. They also gave us USD 150 room credit and addition USD 50 for attending a timeshare presentation. Also during check in they kept a 8000 pesos hold on credit card which was reversed at checkout. An additional 79 pesos was charged per room per day as environmental tax. 
Overall, i'd recommend it and I'll consider going back.

reviews.title: The cost performance is good. The sea is beautiful.
reviews.text: The sea is so beautiful. Last time I went to a not-so-pretty beach in Cancun (Puerto Morelos), I was so surprised, so this time I made sure to choose a hotel with a beautiful beach. The Hotel Zone beach is indeed beautiful. I'm going to relax here. I'm glad I opted for breakfast included. It was stated that breakfast would cost $20 if purchased separately, but when I actually went, it was almost 500 pesos per person. All the food at the restaurant is so delicious. However, you should expect to spend over 1,000 pesos for a meal for two. Even with all-inclusive, I didn't eat much, so having just breakfast included was a good option for me. However, there is only one restaurant open for lunch. For dinner, there is only one restaurant option as well, so it might be a bit tricky if you stay long. They provide two bottles of water in the room every day. The hotel is quiet and peaceful. Since the price is cheap (ocean view with breakfast included is 2,700 pesos per night), I am willing to come back next time. It is only a 15-minute distance from the airport, so it's close. I called an Uber to go to the airport, and it cost about 300 pesos (the hotel taxi is 500 pesos). Even if you call an Uber, they access the hotel lobby easily. You just need to tell them your room number.

Our room was exceptionally clean and quiet the view of the ocean was amazing the pools were warm and clean and inviting, and the music was superb. This was a hidden gem that I am happy to have found. Thanks again to Adriana and to the staff at the Westin spa and resort in Cancun.

reviews.title: Poor staff at reception and housekeeping, definitely avoid in the future!
reviews.positives: The beach is good. It owns regular elements for a beach resort.
reviews.negatives: Sticking to its own rules and totally ignoring customers’ needs! For example, I arrived at noon and was very hungry. I planned to have lunch in the hotel, however the conseige assigned to me was not available until 2pm while other conseige did not answer me because I was not their assigned customer. Another example: although I saw 3-4 cleaning carts on the corridor in the morning, and I vacated the room from 10:20am to 2:30pm, the room was still not cleaned for two nights! The management and working flow definitely have some problems. I have not such issues in other hotels.

Also some of the pools were closed as well as the bars.
Would not recommend.

И недостатков - нет балкона в номере, невозможность открыть полностью окно (конструктивная особенность), изношенная сантехника и мебель в номере. для информации - балконы есть только в номерах на последнем этаже. reviews.title: Not good!!!
reviews.positives: The beach.
reviews.negatives: The hotel staff were not polite, which set a negative tone for our stay. There were also several other downsides. The restaurant options were limited and very expensive — we were paying over $200 per meal for five people. Drinks at the hotel are also pricey; you’re better off walking across the street to OXXO to save money. The beach itself was nice, but getting a palapa was a challenge. If you don’t put a towel down early in the morning, you likely won’t get one. We also experienced what felt like a questionable practice: when we arrived, we were told our room wasn’t ready and were encouraged to upgrade to a more expensive room.

reviews.title: The staff was kind and lovely, but the facility was very below the standard one expects.
reviews.positives:
We were disappointed in the property.  It is a Westin and we expected new and updated facilities.  The entire hotel needs a facelift.  Our room was full of chipped paint and cracks.  There was a flood in our bathroom and the smell was not tolerable.  We had to be moved and they had to take up the entire floor as the water was trapped under it.  
The staff was wonderful and kind, but the facility does not match the quality one would expect from a Westin and price was not commensurate with the product we received.  Even the spa is old and trapped in the 80s and 90s.
reviews.negatives: The entire hotel needs to be updated.

reviews.negatives:
Hariro from concierge bombarded us with coming to a presentation for the direction of the hotel but really what he didn’t say was it was to sell you on Time Shares. Then proceeded to pester us through phone calls(waking us up) and every time we tried to leave the hotel through the lobby. 

Paula the host for breakfast was extremely rude. Embarrassing me first thing in the morning about a dress code (that I hadn’t been told about the previous 6 mornings) then the next day Paula was rude again and the rest of the staff gave me my bill before I even got my breakfast causing me to want to cry.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives:
Took over 2 hours to get checked into a room the front desk staff member, I didn't get his name, but he kept stalling and telling us that our room with a balcony was being cleaned.

After almost 30 minutes of standing at the front desk & no updates in between unless we asked..he continued with the same lie telling us he was waiting to hear back from the cleaning staff. 

We ended up going to grab a bite to eat and when we were done he gave us a key to a different building walking distance..which he didn't tell us about the change.. the bell boy did...after over an hour of waiting we refused to not stay anywhere else but in the main building where our original room was booked. After a manager was requested.. he was no help at all telling us that there were no rooms available in the main building for the next few days..mind you this is not a holiday wknd..so again we refused that excuse bc we knew it was all BS!

 At this point 2hrs later.. wasting half of our day waiting for a room.. we demanded to speak with someone else over the manager..who came and miraculously offered a garden room available in the main building so we opted for that. The room was pretty dated but was in a good location near the pool and beach. 

Food service is only hot quick and fresh when you order it yourself inside of the sports bar. Otherwise you'll be waiting almost 45 minutes just to get served cold food from a pushy waiter waiting for a tip.  Overall I will never return to this hotel at least at this location.

reviews.positives: Room was good size and AC was good. I liked the lagoon view. The servers/staff were good, most of them.
reviews.negatives: Felt trapped there. We wanted to venture out multiple times but it's not straight forward on how to get around. If you taxi you need pesos. If you need pesos you can get some from the front desk, but only $30 worth. That is like a 5 mile round trip in a taxi. We actually told the font desk we're going to the hard rock to check it out and have lunch but was denied access because it is all inclusive. So we did a little taxi ride for $30. If you engage the staff for information you get the constant upsell so we limited that. We did end up using ride share, even though it felt sketchy, but the drivers were good. No english so that was difficult. I found the signage around the facility was lacking, too. I felt nickle and dimes the whole time. Maybe my fault for not doing all inclusive, but got tired of constantly figuring out tips and paperwork.
